timeline.visual_reasoning: Pre-roll. Walking onto mat.
timeline.notes: Pre-roll at Tempo Jiu Jitsu.

timeline.visual_reasoning: Greig (black) and Anthony (red) standing, facing each other. About to engage.
timeline.notes: Facing off. About to start.

timeline.visual_reasoning: Anthony (red) sits to seated guard. Greig (black) standing over him.
timeline.notes: Anthony pulls to seated guard. Greig standing, engaging.
timeline.coaching_tip: Against seated guard: control feet and ankles before committing weight forward.

timeline.visual_reasoning: Greig leaning in, hands on Anthony. Anthony seated with legs active.
timeline.notes: Greig engaging Anthony's guard from standing.
timeline.coaching_tip: Grip knees or ankles first to control leg movement.

timeline.visual_reasoning: Anthony on back, feet pushing into Greig's hips/thighs. Open guard with active feet.
timeline.notes: Anthony playing open guard with active feet on Greig's hips.
timeline.coaching_tip: Use toreando passing — grip both ankles, throw legs to one side.

timeline.visual_reasoning: Anthony pulling Greig down. Legs hooking around Greig's leg. Entering leg entanglement from open guard.
timeline.notes: Anthony entering leg entanglement. Hooking Greig's leg.
timeline.coaching_tip: Backstep immediately when you feel legs hooking — don't let them lock the position.

timeline.visual_reasoning: Both on ground. Legs intertwining symmetrically. Both have one leg wrapped around the other's.
timeline.notes: 50/50 entry. Both legs mirrored and entangled.
timeline.coaching_tip: In 50/50: immediately fight for top position. Control the far knee.

timeline.visual_reasoning: Greig on top within 50/50. Legs still symmetrically entangled but Greig has positional advantage.
timeline.notes: Greig working to get on top within 50/50.
timeline.coaching_tip: Use backstep or leg drag to transition out of 50/50 into a passing position.

timeline.visual_reasoning: Greig base-posting, starting to extract leg from entanglement. Anthony on back.
timeline.notes: Greig beginning to escape 50/50. Working to clear legs.
timeline.coaching_tip: When escaping 50/50: backstep, clear the leg, then immediately pressure into pass.

timeline.visual_reasoning: Greig standing/kneeling over Anthony. Legs clearing. Mid-pass transition.
timeline.notes: Greig extracting from 50/50, transitioning to passing position.
timeline.coaching_tip: Don't pause mid-pass — drive through to completion.

timeline.visual_reasoning: Greig standing over Anthony who is on the ground. Legs between them but not entangled.
timeline.notes: Greig standing over Anthony's open guard. Reset after 50/50.
timeline.coaching_tip: From standing over open guard: toreando or leg drag to pass.

timeline.visual_reasoning: Legs tangling again. Both on ground. Anthony has re-entered the leg entanglement.
timeline.notes: Anthony re-enters 50/50. Second leg entanglement of the roll.
timeline.coaching_tip: Anthony is repeatedly pulling you into 50/50. Need to address this at the entry point — don't let feet hook.

timeline.visual_reasoning: Clear 50/50. Both legs mirrored, symmetrically intertwined. Both on backs/sides.
timeline.notes: 50/50 maintained. Symmetrical leg entanglement.
timeline.coaching_tip: From inside 50/50: look for heel hook opportunities or work the backstep escape.

timeline.visual_reasoning: Greig coming on top within 50/50. Getting his hips above Anthony's.
timeline.notes: Greig winning the top position within 50/50.
timeline.coaching_tip: Good — getting on top is the first step. Now backstep to clear.

timeline.visual_reasoning: Greig on top, Anthony on back. Legs still entangled in 50/50.
timeline.notes: Greig on top of 50/50 but still entangled.
timeline.coaching_tip: Don't stay here — extract the leg and pass immediately.

timeline.visual_reasoning: Greig kneeling up, extracting from entanglement. Anthony starting to recover.
timeline.notes: Greig extracting from 50/50. Transitioning to pass.
timeline.coaching_tip: Speed is key — pass before Anthony can re-establish guard.

timeline.visual_reasoning: Greig on top but one leg still trapped. Half guard. Greig driving forward.
timeline.notes: Greig in top half guard. Nearly completed the pass.
timeline.coaching_tip: Crossface and underhook to flatten Anthony. Then knee slide to complete.

timeline.visual_reasoning: Greig perpendicular across Anthony's chest. Legs cleared. Side control.
timeline.notes: Pass complete! Greig in side control.
timeline.coaching_tip: Now advance — don't stay here. Go to mount or knee on belly within 5 seconds.

timeline.visual_reasoning: Greig maintaining side control. Heavy on Anthony. Good position.
timeline.notes: Side control maintained with good pressure.
timeline.coaching_tip: Look for americana, arm triangle, or advance to mount.

timeline.visual_reasoning: Same position. Greig on top in side control. Anthony being controlled.
timeline.notes: Side control held. Greig consolidating.
timeline.coaching_tip: Don't just hold — attack or advance position.

timeline.visual_reasoning: Anthony starting to create space. Getting a leg back inside. Greig losing control.
timeline.notes: Anthony beginning guard recovery from bottom side control.
timeline.coaching_tip: When they start recovering: switch your hips, re-crossface, kill the space.

timeline.visual_reasoning: Anthony has recovered half guard. One leg back inside. Greig still on top but pass undone.
timeline.notes: Anthony recovers to half guard. First reguard.
timeline.coaching_tip: This is the pattern to fix — consolidate side control faster.

timeline.visual_reasoning: Anthony appears to have recovered to full guard. Legs may be wrapped around Greig. Both on ground chest-to-chest.
timeline.notes: Anthony recovers to closed guard.
timeline.coaching_tip: Stand up to break closed guard. Don't stay kneeling inside.

timeline.visual_reasoning: Greig on top working half guard pass. View partially obscured.
timeline.notes: Back to half guard. Greig working to re-pass.
timeline.coaching_tip: Win the underhook battle from top half guard.

timeline.visual_reasoning: Greig applying pressure from top half guard. Anthony being flattened.
timeline.notes: Greig applying crossface from top half guard.
timeline.coaching_tip: Good pressure — now complete the knee slide.

timeline.visual_reasoning: Greig appears to have completed the pass again. On top controlling Anthony.
timeline.notes: Second guard pass. Greig back in side control.
timeline.coaching_tip: This time ADVANCE immediately — go to mount or knee on belly.

timeline.visual_reasoning: Greig on top. Anthony underneath. Side control or near-side control position.
timeline.notes: Greig maintaining top control.
timeline.coaching_tip: Advance to mount — don't let the reguard cycle repeat.

timeline.visual_reasoning: Anthony getting a leg back in. Half guard recovery happening again.
timeline.notes: Anthony recovers to half guard again. Reguard cycle continues.
timeline.coaching_tip: This is the critical weakness — need to advance to mount before Anthony can recover.

timeline.visual_reasoning: Both on ground in half guard. Greig on top fighting for control.
timeline.notes: Half guard battle. Both fighting for underhooks.
timeline.coaching_tip: If you lose the underhook from top, immediately switch to a knee slice.

timeline.visual_reasoning: Both separated slightly. Greig kneeling, Anthony on back. Space between them.
timeline.notes: Momentary disengage. Both resettling.
timeline.coaching_tip: Use this reset to choose your passing approach.

timeline.visual_reasoning: Greig on top again in a controlling position. Anthony underneath.
timeline.notes: Greig re-establishes top control.
timeline.coaching_tip: Third time in side control — this time advance to mount.

timeline.visual_reasoning: Greig on top in half guard. Working to pass again.
timeline.notes: Half guard again. Greig working the pass.
timeline.coaching_tip: Try a different pass — if knee slide isn't working, try smash pass or backstep.

timeline.visual_reasoning: Both seated/kneeling facing each other. Anthony has butterfly hooks. More upright position.
timeline.notes: Anthony playing butterfly guard. Both more upright.
timeline.coaching_tip: Against butterfly: stay low, control the knees, don't let them get the underhook.

timeline.visual_reasoning: Greig on top with one knee splitting Anthony's guard. Headquarters position. Close to camera.
timeline.notes: Greig in headquarters. Setting up knee slide.
timeline.coaching_tip: Commit to the knee slide — drive across with crossface pressure.

timeline.visual_reasoning: Greig on top, close to camera. Appears to have completed the pass. Driving weight down.
timeline.notes: Pass completed from headquarters. Greig in side control.
timeline.coaching_tip: NOW go to mount. Don't wait.

timeline.visual_reasoning: Greig standing/kneeling over Anthony. Anthony on back with legs active. Reset to open guard.
timeline.notes: Reset to open guard. Anthony recovered again.
timeline.coaching_tip: Passing consolidation remains the main issue.

timeline.visual_reasoning: Anthony has legs wrapped around Greig. Closed guard. Greig inside trying to posture.
timeline.notes: Anthony pulls closed guard. Greig trapped inside.
timeline.coaching_tip: Stand up immediately. Don't accept broken posture in closed guard.

timeline.visual_reasoning: Dynamic movement. Anthony's legs high, Greig being off-balanced. Sweep in progress.
timeline.notes: Anthony sweeps Greig from closed guard!
timeline.coaching_tip: The sweep came from broken posture. Post your hand or leg wide when you feel the off-balance.

timeline.visual_reasoning: Anthony on top. Greig underneath. Sweep completed. Anthony coming over the top.
timeline.notes: Sweep complete. Anthony on top, Greig on bottom.
timeline.coaching_tip: Immediately frame and hip escape. Don't accept bottom position.

timeline.visual_reasoning: Anthony on top controlling Greig. View partially blocked. Anthony driving heavy.
timeline.notes: Anthony consolidating top after the sweep.
timeline.coaching_tip: Frame, shrimp, get knees inside. Don't stay flat.

timeline.visual_reasoning: Greig getting a leg back. Recovering to half guard from bottom.
timeline.notes: Greig recovers to half guard.
timeline.coaching_tip: Good recovery — now get the underhook and work to sweep or reguard fully.

timeline.visual_reasoning: Anthony on top in side control. Greig flat underneath. Anthony has passed.
timeline.notes: Anthony passes Greig's half guard.
timeline.coaching_tip: Don't let yourself get flattened. Turn to side and frame.

timeline.visual_reasoning: Anthony maintaining side control. Greig underneath. Heavy pressure.
timeline.notes: Anthony controlling from side control.
timeline.coaching_tip: Create frames on the neck and hip. Shrimp away.

timeline.visual_reasoning: Anthony has shifted. Head-to-toe position. Greig flat on back with Anthony perpendicular/inverted on top.
timeline.notes: Anthony transitions to north-south.
timeline.coaching_tip: In north-south: frame and spin. Don't let them settle.

timeline.visual_reasoning: Anthony on top. Close view partially blocked. Heavy control.
timeline.notes: Anthony maintaining dominant top.
timeline.coaching_tip: Keep fighting for frames and space.

timeline.visual_reasoning: Anthony kneeling on top of Greig. Appears to be transitioning to mount.
timeline.notes: Anthony advances to mount.
timeline.coaching_tip: Trap and roll or elbow-knee escape immediately.

timeline.visual_reasoning: Anthony (red) close to camera. Greig (black) behind. Open guard battle near end of roll.
timeline.notes: Greig recovers to open guard. Fighting to the end.
timeline.coaching_tip: Good recovery. Keep the legs active.

timeline.visual_reasoning: End of roll. Anthony looking at camera.
timeline.notes: End of roll.

summary.session_overview: 3:44 open mat roll at Tempo Jiu Jitsu. Greig (black) vs Anthony (red). The roll featured two distinct 50/50 leg entanglement exchanges in the first 45 seconds (0:17-0:26 and 0:34-0:46). Greig escaped both and achieved side control but struggled to consolidate — Anthony recovered guard 4+ times between 1:00-2:30. At 2:41, Anthony swept Greig from closed guard and spent the final minute on top advancing through side control, north-south, and mount. Greig showed good recovery at the end.

key_moments.description: Anthony enters 50/50 leg entanglement from seated guard pull.
key_moments.suggestion: You got pulled into 50/50 twice in the first 45 seconds. Control Anthony's feet before engaging his seated guard. When you feel the leg hook, backstep immediately.

key_moments.description: Greig escapes 50/50 and passes to side control.
key_moments.suggestion: Good escape from the entanglement and clean pass. Now the key: advance faster from here.

key_moments.description: Anthony recovers from side control to half guard. First of 4+ reguards.
key_moments.suggestion: This is the main pattern: you pass but don't consolidate. After every pass, advance to mount or knee on belly within 3-5 seconds.

key_moments.description: Anthony sweeps Greig from closed guard — the turning point.
key_moments.suggestion: Got swept because of broken posture in closed guard. Stand up to break closed guard rather than fighting from knees.

key_moments.description: Anthony transitions to north-south on top of Greig.
key_moments.suggestion: After being swept, gave up positions too quickly. Need faster frame-and-shrimp recovery from every bottom position.

top_3_improvements: 50/50 defence: Got pulled into 50/50 twice in the first 45 seconds. When engaging a seated guard player, control feet BEFORE committing weight. When you feel leg hooks, backstep immediately — don't let the entanglement lock.

Passing consolidation: Passed guard 4+ times but couldn't hold side control beyond ~10 seconds. After EVERY pass, advance to mount or knee on belly immediately. Side control is a transition, not a destination. Drill: pass → mount within 3 seconds.

Closed guard posture: Got caught in closed guard and swept from it. Practice standing guard breaks — post one foot, drive hips forward, break the ankles open. Never stay kneeling inside closed guard with broken posture.
strengths_observed: 50/50 escape: Escaped two 50/50 entanglements cleanly by working to top position then extracting — shows good leg entanglement awareness once in the position
Persistent top pressure: Despite Anthony reguarding 4+ times, Greig kept re-passing. Strong work ethic and top pressure.
Passing variety: Used headquarters, knee slide, pressure passing, and toreando approaches. The passing technique itself is solid.
summary.guard_retention_reason: Once swept at 2:41, guard was passed and positions given up relatively quickly (side control → north-south → mount). Limited guard retention from bottom.
summary.positional_awareness_reason: Good awareness of escaping 50/50 and working for top position. Lost points for repeatedly getting caught in closed guard (3x) and not recognising the sweep setup. Post-sweep recovery was slow.
summary.transition_quality_reason: Passes were clean but never consolidated. The transition from side control to mount was never completed in the first 2:30. The 50/50 escapes were smooth. After being swept, transitions on bottom were poor.
summary.overall_notes: This detailed 1-second analysis reveals two clear patterns: (1) Anthony repeatedly pulls Greig into 50/50 from seated guard — this needs to be addressed at the entry point by controlling feet before engaging. (2) The pass → reguard cycle happened 4+ times, which is the #1 area to improve. The solution is simple: treat side control as a 3-second waypoint, not a destination. The moment you achieve side control, immediately advance to mount or knee on belly. Your passing is technically sound — it's the consolidation that needs work. The second half showed the bottom game needs development — being swept from closed guard is a posture issue, and the subsequent position losses (side control → north-south → mount) suggest a need for better framing and hip escape drilling.
